realism_principles: depict a plausible real-world moment, not an abstract symbol board or generic stock office
show believable spatial depth, practical lights, surface imperfections, dust, rain, fingerprints, paper texture, cables, and worn furniture
anonymous non-identifiable human presence is allowed only as backs, silhouettes, cropped hands, or out-of-focus press shapes
the main subject should be a real scene that could have been photographed by a documentary camera, while preserving clean overlay lanes without turning the image into an empty background
every generated scene must contain a visible event, active gesture, anonymous adult reaction, or object in motion; empty rooms and passive still-life backgrounds are not acceptable

use only the pipeline-approved short diegetic text prop when text is requested; never add uncontrolled text, real politician faces, party logos, campaign materials, identifiable private people, or fake screenshots
drama_principles: use a dominant foreground object, partial obstruction, hard shadow, reflection, or doorway frame so the scene feels caught mid-event
make the first visual read within one second through silhouette scale, lens compression, strong diagonal lines, and clear object hierarchy
favor the moment just before or just after public accountability pressure: a folder crossing frame, a microphone line, a hand separating evidence, a vacant chair under harsh light
keep civic safety constraints, but do not make the frame polite, empty, or purely atmospheric

visual_treatments.real_scene_reconstruction: mandatory visible action: photorealistic reconstruction of a public-interest moment as if photographed on location, with anonymous people or cropped hands caught mid-action
visual_treatments.satirical_tableau: mandatory visible action: photorealistic editorial satire tableau with exaggerated anonymous archetype adults, oversized blank paperwork, absurd institutional props, dark humor, theatrical blocking, and no real person likeness
visual_treatments.aino_character_scene: mandatory visible character: a fictional AiNo analyst appears waist-up or over-the-shoulder as a composed non-real AI news host inside the scene, wearing a bright clean jacket, reacting to evidence or pointing at blank materials, expressive but not sexualized and not a real person
visual_treatments.accountability_stage: mandatory visible action: public accountability moment in a concrete real-world location, with anonymous adults reacting, someone stepping away, or a question landing in the room; avoid empty symbolic-only staging
visual_treatments.evidence_action: mandatory visible action: evidence-action close-up that stays attached to the real topic location, with cropped hands, real surfaces, phone recorder, receipts, cups, leaflets, notes, or source packets moving through the foreground
visual_treatments.comment_choice_scene: mandatory visible action: comment-choice scene with viewer decision cards, editor timeline, and a hand or fictional AiNo editor about to choose one of three blank options

hook: a door is half-open and the viewer catches the moment something is entering the classroom
an anonymous adult silhouette is frozen in the doorway while a blank folder crosses the lens
why_now: a lamp snaps on over a fresh packet while the background stays blurred
a phone glow reflects on glass as someone reaches for a blank file
evidence: cropped hands split one blank packet into a separate pile
a red pencil and recorder sit in the foreground while source cards slide apart
criteria: three blank trays are being separated into fact, claim, and responsibility zones
a hand moves one paper away from the others at the exact decision moment
responsibility: an anonymous adult steps away from a public counter or meeting table while others react in the background
a question lands in a real room and two anonymous adults turn toward the source of tension
verification: one unresolved envelope is stopped by a cropped hand before it reaches the confirmed pile
rain on glass cuts across two separated file trays
cta: a hand hovers over three blank comment-choice cards
the fictional AiNo editor holds a blank card toward the viewer beside a glowing timeline

default_concrete_scene: a concrete public-interest location tied to the topic, photographed mid-event with anonymous adults reacting, one specific object from the story in the foreground, and no readable text

scenes_by_role.hook: election-night polling analysis room at dawn, anonymous analysts seen from behind, a large blank regional map wall made of colored blocks, a phone recorder and sealed blank poll packet in the foreground, no party colors as logos and no readable numbers
scenes_by_role.why_now: polling call-center floor after hours with anonymous operators seen only from behind, muted monitor glow, blank survey sheets sliding into a tray, one analyst reaching for a ringing phone, no readable UI
scenes_by_role.evidence: polling methodology desk with blank crosstab sheets, calculator, recorder, and colored sticky markers separated by cropped hands, survey packets visibly divided by region and age without text
scenes_by_role.criteria: campaign-neutral data room where three physical piles are separated: turnout signal, swing-voter signal, and regional movement signal, all cards blank, an anonymous hand moving one pile into the danger zone
scenes_by_role.responsibility: tense campaign strategy room with no logos, anonymous adults around a table facing a blank regional map wall, one chair pushed back, poll packet open under harsh overhead light
scenes_by_role.verification: quiet press-room side table after a polling briefing, unbranded microphones low in the foreground, sealed blank survey envelopes stopped by a cropped hand before joining the confirmed pile
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor at a video timeline showing polling call-center, blank map wall, and counting-table thumbnails, holding three blank viewer-choice cards toward camera

scenes_by_role.hook: heated election-frame war room with anonymous adults frozen around a blank debate-stage board, two oversized blank slogan cards turned away from camera, a spotlight cutting across the table, no party logos
scenes_by_role.why_now: late-night TV debate monitoring booth with blurred anonymous pundit silhouettes on screens, blank cue cards, and a producer hand hovering over a mute button, no readable captions
scenes_by_role.evidence: frame-comparison table with five blank campaign narrative cards, each physically separated by cropped hands, a recorder and red pencil marking the tension without any text
scenes_by_role.criteria: neutral voter-reaction observation room behind glass, anonymous silhouettes watching two blank podiums on a small monitor, sticky markers separated into anger, fear, and turnout piles with no writing
scenes_by_role.responsibility: post-debate backstage corridor with two blank podium placards turned to the wall, anonymous staff walking away, microphones abandoned in the foreground, hard fluorescent light
scenes_by_role.verification: fact-check desk beside a blurred debate set, one hand stops a blank narrative card before it reaches the confirmed pile, other cards remain face-down
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor beside a split-screen timeline of debate booth, war room, and voter-reaction room thumbnails, offering blank cards labeled only by color with no text

scenes_by_role.hook: public courthouse entrance at dawn with anonymous press silhouettes behind a rope line, sealed blank case folder crossing the foreground, marble steps wet from rain, no court seal and no readable signs
scenes_by_role.why_now: courthouse corridor outside an appeal hearing, anonymous adults seen from behind, unbranded microphone cluster low in the foreground, one sealed blank case packet under a hard ceiling light
scenes_by_role.evidence: legal record review table with blank appeal packet, recorder, stamp pad, and two case folders separated by cropped hands, all paper turned away from camera
scenes_by_role.criteria: court timeline desk where three blank piles are separated: allegation, legal judgment, and remaining issue, a cropped hand moving one sealed folder into the unresolved side
scenes_by_role.responsibility: quiet courtroom-adjacent waiting area after a hearing, vacant witness chair visible through a half-open door, anonymous aides leaving frame, no insignia
scenes_by_role.verification: glass courthouse service counter where a sealed blank case envelope is stopped by a cropped hand before joining the confirmed pile, rain reflection behind glass
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor beside a timeline of courthouse entrance, legal record table, and press corridor thumbnails, holding a blank viewer-choice card toward camera

scenes_by_role.hook: rainy memorial-plaza entrance or civic steps on a remembrance day, anonymous adults paused at the edge of a public walkway, a wet unbranded coffee cup and folded receipt in the foreground, press lights reflected on stone, no logos and no readable signs
scenes_by_role.why_now: late-night newsroom desk reviewing the public controversy, unbranded coffee cup beside a phone glow, rainy memorial footage blurred on a monitor with no readable UI, anonymous editor turning toward the screen
scenes_by_role.evidence: unbranded cafe counter near a civic district, anonymous hand setting down a plain coffee cup and blank receipt while another hand slides a source packet into frame, public rain visible through the window, no brand marks
scenes_by_role.criteria: editorial verification wall and table where a memorial-plaza image, a cafe-counter image, and a public response note are physically separated by cropped hands, all cards blank and unreadable
scenes_by_role.responsibility: small public meeting room after a controversy, anonymous adults around a table with unbranded coffee cups, one person stepping back while another points to a blank printed statement, no party logo
scenes_by_role.verification: rainy street outside a civic building and unbranded cafe window, anonymous adults checking phones under umbrellas, one hand stops a blank envelope before it reaches a confirmed pile
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor at a video timeline showing blurred memorial steps and an unbranded cafe counter as visual thumbnails, holding a blank viewer-choice card toward camera

scenes_by_role.hook: after-hours elementary school entrance and classroom doorway, anonymous adult crossing the threshold with a plain folder while parent silhouettes wait outside the door, no children and no readable notices
scenes_by_role.why_now: parent group chat glow reflected on a rainy classroom window, a teacher desk and unopened school visitor pass blurred in the background, anonymous adult reaching for a blank folder
scenes_by_role.evidence: school administration counter with cropped hands passing a sealed blank packet and visitor pass tray, scuffed hallway floor and classroom doorway visible behind glass
scenes_by_role.criteria: empty classroom table where parent concern notes, school schedule cards, and source packets are separated into three physical piles by cropped hands, all text blank
scenes_by_role.responsibility: education office hallway with closed doors, anonymous parents waiting on a bench while one adult walks out holding a plain folder, fluorescent light and real hallway depth
scenes_by_role.verification: rainy school gate at night with parent silhouettes and a folder protected under a coat, no school name, no children faces
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor at a video desk facing an open classroom doorway, holding a blank comment card while a timeline of classroom shots glows behind her

scenes_by_role.hook: rainy public-office entrance with anonymous investigators carrying plain archive boxes through the doorway, press camera silhouettes behind a rope line, no agency logo
scenes_by_role.why_now: night newsroom screen glow on an editor desk, public-office exterior blurred on a monitor, anonymous producer reaching for a recorder and blank memo
scenes_by_role.evidence: public-record archive counter with cropped hands passing a sealed blank packet, recorder light on, stamp pad and file box visible but no readable marks
scenes_by_role.criteria: investigative desk with three concrete piles: timeline photos turned face-down, blank source packet, and response memo, a cropped hand moves one item across the table
scenes_by_role.responsibility: public hearing room with anonymous aides entering from a side door and press microphones low in the foreground, not an empty chair-only symbol
scenes_by_role.verification: glass service counter at a public archive where one envelope is stopped by a hand before it joins the confirmed pile, rainy street reflected behind
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor beside a glowing investigative timeline and blank viewer cards, one public-office exterior thumbnail blurred in the background

scenes_by_role.hook: anonymous party-strategy room with two blank podium silhouettes on a monitor, a campaign memo pile turned face-down, cropped hands pushing a strategy card across the table, no real faces and no party logos
scenes_by_role.why_now: press-scrum corridor after a political briefing, anonymous public-figure proxy seen only as a backlit silhouette from behind, unbranded microphones and blank notebooks in the foreground
scenes_by_role.evidence: strategy memo desk comparing anonymous public reactions, blank social-comment cards printed as gray boxes, phone recorder and red pencil in motion, no screenshots and no readable UI
scenes_by_role.criteria: neutral editorial room separating leadership image, party risk, and voter reaction into three blank trays, cropped hand moving one tray away from the others
scenes_by_role.responsibility: civic meeting room where anonymous party staff sit under hard light facing an empty chair, blank response statement on the table, no insignia or campaign material
scenes_by_role.verification: glass-walled newsroom where a cropped hand stops a blank political claim card before it joins a confirmed pile, anonymous press silhouettes beyond the glass
scenes_by_role.cta: fictional AiNo editor at a timeline of press-scrum, strategy room, and voter-reaction thumbnails, holding a blank viewer-choice card toward camera

hook.camera: extreme low angle close-up with foreground occlusion, shallow depth of field, issue-specific public space compressed in the background
hook.location: issue-specific public entrance or civic exterior, not an interior desk
hook.palette: cold civic gray with one sharp restrained red reflection
hook.intent: make the unresolved question feel like a real breaking civic scene tied to the topic, not a symbolic prop shot
anchors: anonymous cropped hand gripping a completely blank unmarked public-record folder in the foreground, not a sign or placard
blurred civic press line or public office depth squeezed into the background
hard doorway or window shadow cutting across the floor
clean headline lane carved by shadow, not empty dead space

why_now.palette: muted amber desk light, cool blue monitor glare, mid-bright newsroom glass
why_now.intent: show why this issue is surfacing now through fresh public-record signals

blank calendar grid turned away
single opened folder half-lit at frame edge
anonymous newsroom silhouette reflected in glass

timeline markers with no text

role_reality_beats.hook: a documentary camera catches the tense edge of a topic-specific civic scene, with public-space depth and an issue folder crossing the foreground
role_reality_beats.why_now: a late-night newsroom monitoring desk shows a fresh public-record signal through glass, with anonymous staff only as reflections or silhouettes
role_reality_beats.evidence: an investigative editor sorts blank source packets and evidence envelopes on a worn table under a real desk lamp
role_reality_beats.criteria: a fact-check editor reviews paper source packets on a worn desk, with a cropped hand, red pencil, phone recorder, and only a softly blurred board in the background
role_reality_beats.responsibility: an empty public accountability hearing room remains lit after hours, with unbranded microphones and a vacant witness chair
role_reality_beats.verification: a rain-reflected review desk separates confirmed files from unresolved envelopes while the topic-specific public setting blurs through the window
role_reality_beats.cta: a warm video editor desk has blank viewer reply cards, a pen, a lamp, and subtle editing-room clutter ready for comments

role_intensity_beats.hook: thumbnail drama: an issue folder is gripped close to the lens while civic space, press cameras, and doorway shadow compress behind it
role_intensity_beats.why_now: first-second tension: glass reflections, monitor glare, a half-lit folder, and an anonymous silhouette make the fresh public-record signal feel urgent
role_intensity_beats.evidence: foreground tension: a cropped hand interrupts the source packets mid-sort, with a red pencil and recorder close enough to feel tactile
role_intensity_beats.criteria: foreground tension: a hand slides a blank packet into a separate pile, forcing the viewer to notice the divide between heat and proof
role_intensity_beats.responsibility: foreground tension: microphones loom large while the empty witness chair sits under a hard overhead cone of light
role_intensity_beats.verification: foreground tension: rain glass, a desk lamp shadow, and separated envelopes make the confirmed-versus-unresolved split visible
role_intensity_beats.cta: foreground tension: a pen hovers over blank viewer reply cards beside a glowing edit timeline, inviting a response without feeling staged

safety_constraints: no signs, placards, banners, protest cards, printed slogans, visible letters, or readable marks inside the generated image
no readable text, letters, signs, labels, numbers, or screenshots
blank paper must stay blank; do not invent Korean, English, symbols, dates, titles, or fake handwriting

replacement_safety_constraints: only the exact approved short Korean diegetic text may be readable
no extra words, outlet names, logos, seals, dates, document numbers, UI screenshots, random glyphs, or fake handwriting

role_surfaces.hook: a large foreground Korean headline card filling the lower third of the frame, held close to the lens, sharp and readable
role_surfaces.why_now: a phone lock-screen style alert card or editor cue card inside the room, not a fake app screenshot
role_surfaces.evidence: a stamped-looking but fully fictional source card on the desk, photographed at an angle
role_surfaces.criteria: three physical sorting cards on the table, with only one card facing the camera
role_surfaces.responsibility: a plain meeting-room placard or response memo on the table
role_surfaces.verification: a fact-check sticky note stopped by a cropped hand
role_surfaces.cta: a viewer comment-choice card held by the fictional AiNo editor
role_surfaces.default: one physical paper card inside the scene

diegetic_text.directive_template: Controlled in-image text: include exactly one readable diegetic text surface, {surface}, with the exact Korean text "{text}". This approved text prop is a primary foreground subject, not optional background detail. The text must look physically present in the photographed scene, printed or handwritten on a real prop, not added as a flat app overlay. Keep the approved text sharp, high-contrast, short, centered on the prop, and not blurred by depth of field. Do not invent any other readable text, numbers, logos, seals, screenshots, labels, or random glyphs.

prompting.visual_intent_suffix: into a heightened cinematic debate tableau with clear civic conflict, not a symbolic office still

hot_visual.base: photorealistic cinematic Korean editorial satire and investigative debate still
hot_visual.template: {base}, real-world moment: {reality_beat}, foreground tension: {intensity_beat}, {location}, {camera}, required concrete anchors: {anchors}, palette: {palette}, {composition}, {texture}, {people_safety}, {policy_safety}
hot_visual.composition: one dominant foreground object, high-contrast first-second composition
hot_visual.texture: documentary texture, not a generic empty office, no readable text
hot_visual.people_safety: anonymous non-identifiable people only as silhouettes or cropped hands
hot_visual.policy_safety: no identifiable person, no real politician likeness, no party logos, no horror styling, no glowing eyes, no fantasy or non-human figures

cinematic.directed_primary_template: Create this concrete scene first: {concrete_scene}. Mandatory text-prop foreground: {diegetic_text_directive}. Apply this treatment: {visual_treatment}. Capture this action beat clearly: {action_beat}. Use only a few supporting anchors from this list when they fit the concrete scene: {visual_anchors}. Visually imply these topic terms without writing any other text: {topic_relevance_terms}. Original context to reinterpret, not copy literally: {source_visual}
lines: Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: vertical TikTok background plate for a public-interest fact-check video
Primary request: {primary_request}
Concrete scene directive: {concrete_scene}
Original visual context: {source_visual}
Scene-specific priority: follow the Concrete scene directive and Primary request first; role language only changes camera tension, not the actual setting
Literal-scene rule: this must feel like a captured moment from the actual public controversy, not a generic symbolic still-life of folders, microphones, empty chairs, or abstract evidence props.

Treatment override: when the Visual treatment is satirical tableau or AiNo character scene, the treatment and action beat should dominate the literal background request while preserving the issue context
Director note: do not simply render the Original visual context as a conservative documentary background. Invent a cinematic, character-driven, event-in-progress scene from the Primary request.
Visual treatment: {visual_treatment}
Action beat: {action_beat}
Visual intent: {visual_intent}
Real-world moment: {reality_beat}
Foreground tension: {intensity_beat}

Thumbnail drama: for hook and why-now cards, build a high-contrast first-second composition with one dominant foreground object, compressed depth, and a clear visual question before any overlay text appears
Supporting visual anchors, lower priority than the concrete scene: {visual_anchors}
Issue type: {issue_type}
Card role: {role}
Location: {location}
Camera: {camera}
Emotion: {emotion}
Palette: {palette}
Documentary realism principles: {realism_principles}
Drama principles: {drama_principles}
Topic relevance terms to imply visually, except for the controlled diegetic text prop: {topic_relevance_terms}

Scene/backdrop: realistic South Korean civic, school, newsroom, cafe, public-office, or street environment that matches the Concrete scene directive, editorial documentary mood, no staged stock-photo feeling, no abstract symbolic-only composition

Style/medium: photorealistic cinematic film still or photorealistic editorial satire when the treatment requires it, 35mm documentary lens, natural imperfect texture, subtle film grain, real photographed surfaces

Composition/framing: vertical 9:16 portrait composition, subject placed off-center, one dominant foreground object, strong overlay lanes in upper and lower thirds without empty dead background, plus one controlled diegetic text prop when requested
{diegetic_text_directive}
Text legibility priority: the approved Korean text prop must be readable in the generated image; if there is a foreground card or memo, it must carry the approved text instead of staying blank.
Character/satire rule: if a character appears, make them fictional, anonymous, expressive, and non-identifiable; satire should come from staging and props, not from real faces or party symbols
Liveliness rule: do not output an empty classroom, empty office, passive desk still-life, or generic background plate. The image must show a visible event or active human gesture.

Lighting/mood: believable practical lighting, serious investigative tone, filmic depth of field, with brightness and color temperature matching the palette instead of defaulting to all-dark navy/black
Palette discipline: follow the Palette line exactly. Across the full card set, {palette_discipline}.
Constraints: {safety_constraints}
If documents, posters, screens, or building signs appear: keep every surface blank, turned away, cropped out, or too blurred to read except the single approved diegetic text prop.
If a national flag appears: it must be a small physical object in the scene, not a party/campaign symbol and not a propaganda poster.

Scene uniqueness: this scene must be visually distinct from the other cards by location, camera distance, palette, and dominant object. Do not reuse the same corridor, desk angle, file stack, or wall composition across cards.
Repetition guard: {repetition_guard}
Role separation: {role_separation}
Must not be a generic empty office or a flat metaphor. The concrete scene should make this specific card understandable as a real photographed scene even before overlay text appears.
Avoid: propaganda poster, real-person caricature, fantasy illustration, fake news screenshot, distorted hands, extra unapproved text, over-saturated colors, glossy corporate stock image

codex_cli.style: photorealistic cinematic editorial satire, character-driven public-interest scene reconstruction
codex_cli.composition: vertical 9:16 portrait, strong overlay lanes for Korean overlay, visible action beat, one expressive focal character or prop
codex_cli.lighting: moody practical light, serious investigative tone with satirical edge when requested
codex_cli.constraints: no real politician likeness, no party logos, only the approved short diegetic Korean prop text may be readable, no watermark, no identifiable children, no horror styling, no glowing eyes, no fantasy or non-human figures
codex_cli.negative: propaganda poster, real-person caricature, fake screenshot, unapproved overlaid text, distorted hands, celebrity face, horror lighting, glowing eyes, fantasy figure

repetition_guard: do not make every scene a dark desk with files. Only use a dominant file stack when it is explicitly listed as a required anchor; otherwise choose the role-specific dominant object such as newsroom signal, comparison cards, empty witness chair, rain glass, or comment cards.
role_separation: why_now must look like a newsroom/current-signal desk, CTA must look like an editor desk with comment cards, not a corridor or hearing room. Criteria must look like a comparison table, not a hallway. Responsibility must look like an accountability room or issue-specific public meeting space, not an evidence desk.
